decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful creature for a low cost. It utilizes card draw and counterspells to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to control the game tempo while setting up for a quick win through the combo. With a focus on efficiency and precision, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through careful planning and execution of the combo.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly establish a board presence through aggressive creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Wild Mongrel while efficiently removing threats with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The deck also utilizes Survival of the Fittest to tutor for key creatures like Quirion Dryad and Arrogant Wurm. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy, with a mix of Mountains and Forests along with lands like Forest','','');">Karplusan Forest and Wasteland. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast and powerful threats while disrupting their game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to assemble a powerful enchantress engine by playing enchantments that draw cards whenever you play an enchantment, while also ramping up mana with cards like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing, protecting key pieces such as Opalescence and Parallax Wave. The ultimate goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of enchantments that generate card advantage and powerful threats.

This Goblin aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures. It aims to quickly build a board presence with cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief, while utilizing cards like Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Matron to maintain card advantage and search for key pieces. The deck also has disruptive elements with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to hinder the opponent's mana base. The inclusion of Siege-Gang Commander and Goblin Piledriver provides additional reach and damage potential, making it a powerful and fast-paced deck in the Premodern 1 vs 1 format.

This Mono Black Aggro deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and aggressively with low cost creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Knight of Stromgald. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Cabal Therapy, it can disrupt opponents' strategies and maintain pressure on the board. The inclusion of control elements like Smother and Diabolic Edict helps to deal with opposing threats, while cards like Graveborn Muse provide card advantage to keep the aggression going. With a solid mana base and versatile spells like Wasteland and Cursed Scroll, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in the early game.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat on the board. The deck includes a mix of counter spells, card draw, and manipulation to help find and protect the combo pieces. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while efficiently executing the Dreadnought-Stifle combo to overwhelm them and secure a win.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana and disrupting the opponent's lands with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland. It utilizes aggressive creatures like Terravore, Nimble Mongoose, and Werebear to quickly pressure the opponent while also providing card advantage through Sylvan Library. The inclusion of Weathered Wayfarer helps find specific lands, while cards like Call of the Herd and Treetop Village provide additional threats. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early on and keep them on the back foot throughout the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing disruptive spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress to control the opponent's hand while also utilizing removal spells like Diabolic Edict and Smother to control the board. Pernicious Deed acts as a versatile board wipe, while Wall of Roots and Wall of Blossoms provide defense and card advantage. The deck also utilizes mana denial with Dust Bowl and ramp with Yavimaya Elder. Overall, the main goal is to control the game and outvalue the opponent using a combination of disruption, removal, and card advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to create a powerful and synergistic engine with enchantments, particularly through the Argothian Enchantress and Enchantress's Presence. The deck aims to generate card advantage and mana acceleration through enchantments like Wild Growth and Serra's Sanctum, while controlling the board with cards like Parallax Wave and Solitary Confinement. The deck also includes recursion effects like Replenish to bring back key enchantments from the graveyard. Overall, the deck focuses on creating a strong enchantment-based combo strategy to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The core idea of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle, allowing you to cheat the Dreadnought into play for just one mana. By disrupting your opponent with counterspells and card draw, you aim to control the game until you can assemble your combo and win the game in a decisive manner. The deck's synergy lies in the efficient use of stifle effects to counter your opponent's threats while setting up your own game-winning plays.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing counter spells, removal, and card advantage to stall the opponent's game plan. The key strategy is to establish a defensive board presence with Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave while using Standstill to force the opponent into difficult decisions. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, it sets up for powerful finishers like Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice to close out the game. The combination of control elements and land denial with Dust Bowl ensures dominance in the late game.

The deck focuses on controlling and slowing down the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal spells, and board wipes while maintaining card advantage with cards like Standstill and Fact or Fiction. By using a combination of control elements and land denial tactics, it aims to stall the game and ultimately outvalue the opponent through efficient resource management and card draw. The deck can also win through incremental damage from manlands like Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave if the game goes long.

This deck revolves around using Ill-Gotten Gains and various other spells to fuel a powerful combo. By using cards like Brain Freeze and Cabal Ritual in combination with cards that allow for intense card draw and mana generation, the deck aims to quickly fill its graveyard and cast a game-winning spell. The deck is built to optimize its combo potential and disrupt the opponent's strategy, ensuring a swift victory.

This BW Control deck focuses on disruption and removal spells to control the opponent's board. It includes a mix of hand disruption like Duress and Cabal Therapy, creature removal with Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate, and board wipes like Wrath of God and Decree of Justice. The deck also includes cards for card advantage like Skeletal Scrying and Gerrard's Verdict, as well as for late-game value with Eternal Dragon. The strategy is to stall the game and slowly gain advantage until the opponent is overwhelmed.

This deck focuses on using Stasis to lock down your opponent's resources while efficiently drawing through your deck with cards like Impulse and Gush. Counterspells and bounce spells help protect your combo pieces and disrupt your opponent's plays. The goal is to create a lock that stops your opponent from being able to untap their lands, all while putting pressure on them with cards like Black Vise. This deck requires careful resource management and timing to execute its game plan effectively.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Knight of Stromgald and Hypnotic Specter, backed up by disruptive spells like Duress and Cabal Therapy. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Wasteland, the deck aims to establish board control early on and maintain pressure with its low-cost, efficient creatures. The inclusion of Graveborn Muse provides card advantage to keep the aggression going, while cards like Smother and Plaguebearer help deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to win the game quickly through aggressive, efficient plays.
